tex:
(a) This table shows the temperature, in °C, at midnight and at 3 pm for four cities on the same day.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline
City & Temperature at midnight (°C) & Temperature at 3 pm (°C) \\
\hline
Sydney & 21 & 28 \\
Oslo & -3 & 1 \\
Toronto & -18 & -8 \\
Seoul & -5 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Use the table to complete this statement.
\newline

The city with the biggest difference in temperature between midnight and 3 pm

is  with a difference of °C.
\newline

(b) The temperature at midnight in Moscow was -11 °C.
\newline
At 3 pm the temperature has increased by 5 °C.
\newline

Work out the temperature at 3 pm.
\newline

 °C

content.content:
This table shows the temperature, in °C, at midnight and at 3 pm for four cities on the same day.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline
City & Temperature at midnight (°C) & Temperature at 3 pm (°C) \\
\hline
Sydney & 21 & 28 \\
Oslo & -3 & 1 \\
Toronto & -18 & -8 \\
Seoul & -5 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Use the table to complete this statement.
\newline

The city with the biggest difference in temperature between midnight and 3 pm

is .................................................. with a difference of ................................°C.
\newline

The temperature at midnight in Moscow was -11 °C.
\newline
At 3 pm the temperature has increased by 5 °C.
\newline

Work out the temperature at 3 pm.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item This table shows the temperature, in °C, at midnight and at 3 pm for four cities on the same day.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline
City & Temperature at midnight (°C) & Temperature at 3 pm (°C) \\
\hline
Sydney & 21 & 28 \\
Oslo & -3 & 1 \\
Toronto & -18 & -8 \\
Seoul & -5 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Use the table to complete this statement.

The city with the biggest difference in temperature between midnight and 3 pm

is .................................................. with a difference of ................................°C.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item The temperature at midnight in Moscow was -11 °C.
At 3 pm the temperature has increased by 5 °C.

Work out the temperature at 3 pm.

\AnswerLine[] °C
\Marks{1}

\end{parts}
